description: Customer-specific declarative plugins providing MCP tools and HTTP routes, loaded at runtime by plugin_loader via importlib

key_symbols.name: name
key_symbols.description: Plugin name '_example', version 0.1.0, mode 'mock', installer 'upstream'

key_symbols.name: mcp_tools
key_symbols.description: Declares 2 MCP tools: example_echo (tools.echo) and example_lookup (tools.lookup) with input schemas

key_symbols.name: http_routes
key_symbols.description: Declares 2 HTTP routes: POST /api/example/echo (routes.post_echo) and GET /api/example/{record_id} (routes.get_record)

key_symbols.name: mock_server
key_symbols.description: Seed data from mock_data/examples.json, database at .autoservice/database/_example/mock.db

key_symbols.name: references
key_symbols.description: Lists references/README.md as a reference for the AI agent

key_symbols.name: _get_tools
key_symbols.description: Lazy-imports the tools module from sys.modules['autoservice.plugins._example.tools'] (registered by plugin_loader)

key_symbols.name: post_echo
key_symbols.description: Async POST /api/example/echo handler; delegates to tools.echo(message=message)

key_symbols.name: get_record
key_symbols.description: Async GET /api/example/{record_id} handler; delegates to tools.lookup(id=record_id)

key_symbols.name: _RECORDS
key_symbols.description: In-memory dict of 2 example records (EX-001: Acme Corp, EX-002: Globex Inc) with id/name/status fields

key_symbols.name: echo
key_symbols.description: Async MCP tool handler; returns {'echo': message} for the given input message

key_symbols.name: lookup
key_symbols.description: Async MCP tool handler; looks up a record by ID from _RECORDS, returns {'record': ...} or {'error': ...}

key_symbols.name: customers
key_symbols.description: Array of 2 customer records (EX-001 Acme Corp enterprise, EX-002 Globex Inc new) with id/name/phone/email/type/company/industry fields

key_symbols.name: References documentation
key_symbols.description: Explains that references/ holds glossaries, product docs, pricing tables, FAQs, and policy documents for the AI agent

key_symbols.name: name
key_symbols.description: Plugin name 'cinnox', version 1.0.0, mode 'mock', installer 'cinnox'

key_symbols.name: mcp_tools
key_symbols.description: Declares 4 MCP tools: cinnox_customer_lookup, cinnox_billing, cinnox_subscriptions, cinnox_permission_check

key_symbols.name: http_routes
key_symbols.description: Declares 3 HTTP routes: GET customers/{identifier}, GET customers/{account_id}/billing, GET customers/{account_id}/subscriptions

key_symbols.name: mock_server
key_symbols.description: Seed data from mock_data/accounts.json, database at .autoservice/database/cinnox/mock.db

key_symbols.name: references
key_symbols.description: Lists glossary.json and synonym-map.json as reference files for the AI agent

key_symbols.name: _get_tools
key_symbols.description: Lazy-imports the tools module from sys.modules['autoservice.plugins.cinnox.tools'] (registered by plugin_loader)

key_symbols.name: get_customer
key_symbols.description: Async GET /api/cinnox/customers/{identifier} handler; delegates to tools.customer_lookup(identifier)

key_symbols.name: get_billing
key_symbols.description: Async GET /api/cinnox/customers/{account_id}/billing handler; delegates to tools.billing_query with optional start_date/end_date

key_symbols.name: get_subscriptions
key_symbols.description: Async GET /api/cinnox/customers/{account_id}/subscriptions handler; delegates to tools.subscription_query(account_id)

key_symbols.name: _db
key_symbols.description: Module-level Optional[MockDB] instance, injected by plugin_loader at load time

key_symbols.name: _get_db
key_symbols.description: Returns the shared MockDB instance; raises RuntimeError if not initialized

key_symbols.name: set_db
key_symbols.description: Sets the global _db MockDB instance for all tools in this module

key_symbols.name: _envelope
key_symbols.description: Wraps response data/error in standard API envelope dict with success/data/error/timestamp/mode fields

key_symbols.name: customer_lookup
key_symbols.description: MCP tool: looks up CINNOX customer by identifier via db.get_customer(); logs API call

key_symbols.name: billing_query
key_symbols.description: MCP tool: gets billing history via db.get_billing() with optional date range; logs API call

key_symbols.name: subscription_query
key_symbols.description: MCP tool: gets active subscriptions via db.get_subscriptions(); logs API call

key_symbols.name: permission_check
key_symbols.description: MCP tool: checks operator permissions via check_permission() from autoservice.permission; supports product-specific permissions

key_symbols.name: accounts array
key_symbols.description: Array of 6 CINNOX customer account records (ACC-1001 through ACC-6006) with id/name/company/email/phone/type/plan/seats/dids/integrations/billing_status/monthly_amount/notes

key_symbols.name: glossary object
key_symbols.description: Large JSON object mapping ~350 CINNOX/telecom domain terms to descriptions and related terms (e.g. 1:1 Call, ACD, DID, IVR, PSTN, WhatsApp BSP)

key_symbols.name: synonym map object
key_symbols.description: JSON object mapping 35 abbreviations/synonyms to canonical telecom terms (e.g. acd->Average Call Duration, did->DID, ivr->Interactive Voice Response)

interfaces.name: billing_query(account_id: str, start_date: str = None, end_date: str = None) -> dict
permission_check(action: str, product_id: str = None) -> dict
get_billing(account_id: str, start_date: str = None, end_date: str = None) -> dict

test_results.note: No test files found within plugins/ directory; plugin testing presumably covered by framework-level tests or e2e tests

user_flows.name: MCP tool dispatch (example plugin)
steps: plugin_loader reads plugins/_example/plugin.yaml and discovers mcp_tools
plugin_loader imports plugins/_example/tools.py via importlib, registers as sys.modules['autoservice.plugins._example.tools']
MCP server (feishu/channel.py) registers example_echo and example_lookup tools
On tool call, handler invokes tools.echo() or tools.lookup() which return dict results

user_flows.name: HTTP route dispatch (example plugin)
steps: web/app.py mounts POST /api/example/echo -> routes.post_echo and GET /api/example/{record_id} -> routes.get_record
Route handlers call _get_tools() to lazy-load the tools module from sys.modules
Delegates to tools.echo() or tools.lookup() for actual business logic

user_flows.name: CINNOX customer lookup (MCP + HTTP)
steps: Plugin loader loads plugins/cinnox/plugin.yaml, imports tools.py, calls set_db() to inject MockDB instance
MockDB is seeded from mock_data/accounts.json (6 customer accounts)
MCP tool cinnox_customer_lookup or HTTP GET /api/cinnox/customers/{identifier} is invoked
tools.customer_lookup() queries db.get_customer(identifier), wraps result in _envelope(), logs API call

user_flows.name: CINNOX billing query
steps: MCP tool cinnox_billing or HTTP GET /api/cinnox/customers/{account_id}/billing is invoked
tools.billing_query() resolves customer via db.get_customer(), then calls db.get_billing() with optional date range
Result wrapped in _envelope() and API call logged

user_flows.name: CINNOX permission check
steps: MCP tool cinnox_permission_check is invoked with action and optional product_id
If product_id given, fetches product-specific permissions via db.get_product_full_data()
Calls autoservice.permission.check_permission() with action, product_permissions, domain='customer-service'
